title: orchidocarpum pygmaeum
intro: Orchidocarpum pygmaeum, also known as the dwarf nutmeg tree, is a plant species belonging to the Celastraceae family. It is primarily found in the tropical regions of India, Southeast Asia, and China, where its seeds and leaves are used for medicinal purposes. The plant is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. In traditional herbal medicine, the seeds are often used to prepare teas, decoctions, and infusions, while the leaves can be made into a poultice or added to herbal preparations for its medicinal properties.
